attestation_note: Three errors corrected: (1) Kubernetes recategorized from owned skill to managed-cluster usage — did not author Helm charts or cluster configs; (2) Hibernate/JPA added to skills — used hands-on at Mercedes for query updates during DB2-to-PostgreSQL migration, omitted by agent from skills extraction; (3) Capgemini contribution scope corrected — 'owning design through production deployment' overstated individual ownership on a team of 4–6 engineers, reworded to reflect team context.

experience.title: Software Developer Intern
contributions: Built backend services and frontend features for an AI-driven QA platform, developing REST APIs, execution-monitoring dashboards, and workflow orchestration capabilities
Developed reusable validation services and test templates that transformed AI-generated test specifications into executable test artifacts
Implemented automated testing and CI/CD workflows for builds, testing, and deployments

experience.title: Software Engineer II
contributions: Designed and built a scalable rules engine for automotive configuration and car modification workflows, delivering high-throughput REST APIs with 70% performance improvement — metric measured and owned by me against baseline query benchmarks

Led end-to-end migration of critical applications from IBM DB2 to PostgreSQL — schema conversion, stored-procedure migration, Hibernate query updates (hands-on), Flyway migration scripts, validation checks, and production cutover planning — completing with zero downtime

Built CI/CD pipelines with GitHub Actions and AWS (EC2, S3, RDS), containerized microservices using Docker, and deployed into existing Kubernetes-managed development and staging environments — cluster infrastructure owned by platform team, not authored by me
Collaborated cross-functionally with product and QA teams to define API contracts, conduct code reviews, and maintain technical documentation

experience.title: Software Engineer
contributions: Contributed to microservices and React-based frontend features for enterprise payroll and HR systems as part of a team of 4-6 engineers, participating from design through production deployment across Agile releases
Implemented JWT-based authentication and role-based access control for payroll, employee self-service, and administrative functions
Containerized applications using Docker, contributed to CI/CD pipelines, and refactored legacy modules into maintainable service components

projects.name: SentinelPay - AI Fraud Detection Engine
contributions: Architected a real-time fraud detection platform using a three-layer approach: deterministic rule evaluation, Redis-based behavioral profiling, and Claude AI risk scoring — rule engine and behavioral profiling completing in under 150ms on AWS EC2
Implemented JWT authentication, role-based access control, and REST APIs for merchant onboarding, transaction monitoring, fraud investigation, and risk management

Deployed FastAPI service on AWS EC2 with RDS PostgreSQL, ElastiCache Redis, CloudWatch monitoring with X-Ray distributed tracing, and GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline; implemented 89 unit, integration, and end-to-end tests using pytest

projects.name: Budget Buddy
contributions: Built secure REST APIs with JWT auth and role-based access for expense ingestion and categorization
Designed MongoDB aggregation pipelines for real-time budget summaries, trend alerts, and interactive analytics dashboards
Deployed containerized services using Docker with optimized query indexing for low-latency performance at scale

skills.kubernetes_note: Used managed Kubernetes clusters for dev/staging deployments at Mercedes-Benz — did not author cluster configs or Helm charts
